text: You know, back during the Second World War, the German battleship Graph Spay was cornered by British warships in the neutral port of Monte Vido. Rather than face the humiliation of being captured or sacrificing his crew fighting a hopeless battle, the captain chose instead to take his ship out into the center of the bay and blow the thing up in front of the entire city. Yeah, it was arguably the world's most expensive fireworks display, but it also got me thinking about Starfleet Academy, a show

that looks so bafflingly, mindbendingly, unapologetically terrible that I can only explain its existence as Paramount staging their very own version of the graph spay. I mean, you'd think after the cringe-inducing disasters of discovery and strange new worlds, they'd want to change course and roll out something a little more mature, thoughtful, disciplined, and well, Star Trekish. Nope. It feels almost like a final act of defiance. Like, we know this franchise is completely and utterly

cooked at this point, so what the hell? Let's go out with a bang, shall we?
